title: Paid Advertising Campaign
description: Design a complete paid advertising campaign for Meta (Facebook/Instagram) or Google Ads, including audience targeting, creative, budget allocation, and measurement plan.
whyItMatters: Paid advertising is the fastest way to reach a targeted audience at scale. Understanding how to plan, execute, and measure paid campaigns is a core skill for any digital marketer.

steps.title: Define campaign objective
steps.description: Choose a clear goal: brand awareness, lead generation, or sales. This determines your campaign type, bidding strategy, and success metrics.

steps.title: Define target audience
steps.description: Build your targeting: demographics, interests, behaviours, and lookalike audiences. Create 2-3 audience segments to test.
steps.tips: Start broader than you think — let the algorithm find the best performers within your audience.

steps.title: Create ad concepts
steps.description: Design 3-4 ad variations. For each: headline, primary text, visual/video concept, CTA. Follow platform best practices (Meta: square or vertical, Google: responsive search ads).

steps.title: Plan campaign structure
steps.description: Organise into campaigns > ad sets > ads. Each ad set tests one variable (audience, placement, or creative). Keep it structured.

steps.title: Set budget and bidding
steps.description: Allocate a realistic budget (e.g., $500-$2,000). Split across ad sets. Define bidding strategy (lowest cost, target CPA, etc.). Calculate expected reach and results.

steps.title: Build measurement plan
steps.description: Define KPIs: impressions, CTR, CPC, conversion rate, CPA, ROAS. Set up conversion tracking (pixel or GA4). Build a simple reporting dashboard.

steps.title: Create optimisation plan
steps.description: Plan what you'll check daily/weekly. When will you pause underperformers? When will you scale winners? Define rules.
steps.tips: Don't optimise too early — give each ad set at least 50 conversions before making big changes.

tools: Meta Ads Manager or Google Ads

rubric.description: Campaign has a clear objective tied to business goals, not vanity metrics.
Audiences are well-defined and differentiated for testing.
Ad concepts are compelling, platform-appropriate, and follow best practices.
KPIs are defined, tracking is planned, and optimisation rules are clear.
